note: The git-anchored, MONOTONIC genesis state for the EvaluationContract attestation chain (feature 008, round 5). It is COMMITTED, git-reviewable state that the strict certify path loads and RUNS against — never a caller argument the scored party could forge. Fields: genesis_history_root is the REAL, non-empty history_root of the initial committed cohort (chain_root over its version content hashes) and is the immutable base of the history chain; latest.{history_root,attestation_root} are the most recently certified roots and advance ONLY via contract.advance_committed_root(...), called by the harness after a successful strict certify; evaluator.{id,verify_key_hex} is the committed evaluator identity + verify-key that validate() verifies attestations against (HMAC-SHA256 shared secret here as a deterministic placeholder; PRODUCTION commits an ed25519 PUBLIC key while the private signing key is held by a party that is NOT the scored subject, curator != subject). Strict certification requires the presented history to append-only-EXTEND latest.history_root (else ATTESTATION_NOT_EXTENDING), the attestation to chain from latest.attestation_root (else GENESIS_UNANCHORED), and the attestation to be signed by the committed evaluator key with the committed evaluator id (else ATTESTATION_INVALID). So no operator can silently truncate the cohort or re-anchor the chain to a fresh, private genesis: git history supplies the external timestamp and review a pure validator cannot. To rotate the genesis or advance the chain by hand (a deliberate, reviewable act) commit a new value here; the change is visible in git history.
